hero.access: Listed equities, FX, and property are publicly tradable. Equity ownership in production companies is a different asset class , ORTAQ makes that class readable.

hero.label: Equity partnership · Turkish industrial investment
hero.title: Own shares in production companies.
hero.lead: ORTAQ publishes field-reviewed dossiers on export-oriented Turkish manufacturers. What you buy: equity in a physical production facility. Capital works for capacity, machinery, and export growth.
hero.conviction: Passive assets wait for price. Production companies compound value through capacity, exports, and operational improvement. Equity owners share in that growth. There is no return guarantee; the model is long-horizon industrial partnership.
hero.accessLine: Regulated private-market access. Productive equity partnership; not crowdfunding, not speculation.

opportunities.label: Investment opportunities
opportunities.title: Export-oriented production companies
opportunities.lead: CNC, food, logistics, chemicals, textile. Field-reviewed equity-partnership dossiers across distinct industrial sectors. Each is a real production facility, not a startup card.
opportunities.capitalLead: Every dossier states where the capital goes: line expansion, export infrastructure, machinery upgrade. The money works inside the factory. Operational growth financing, not speculation.

sectors.label: Production economy
sectors.title: Industrial investment · sector diversity
sectors.lead: Each sector carries a distinct capacity, export, and operational-growth story. Unlike passive assets, value is created through production.

process.label: Investment journey
process.title: From dossier review to value creation: six steps
process.subtitle: The individual investor's path: read the dossier, verify it, participate, monitor, and track company performance.

1.title: Dossier review
1.text: Read the investment dossiers: company files documented with production, export, and field notes.

2.title: Structural evaluation
2.text: Examine capacity, risk profile, machine park, capital use plan, and review layers.

3.title: Verification
3.text: Understand the SPK framework, licensed platform, escrow account, and MKK share registry.

4.title: Participation
4.text: When ready, acquire shares via the licensed platform; capital remains in escrow until the campaign closes.

5.title: Monitoring
5.text: Track your partnership through field updates, operational traces, and company reports.

6.title: Value creation
6.text: Capacity growth, export expansion, margin improvement: company value develops with operational performance. Dividends, appreciation, or exit vary by campaign; no guarantee.

valueChain.label: Value formation
valueChain.title: From capital to investor upside: a measurable chain
valueChain.lead: Your capital goes to tangible growth on the factory floor, not speculative price movement. Value forms through operational performance , trackable and reportable.

1.title: Capital
1.text: Investment is collected in escrow; campaign target and use-of-funds plan are stated in the dossier.

2.title: Production
2.text: Funds deploy to machinery, capacity, lines, or export infrastructure: physical assets expand.

3.title: Growth
3.text: Output volume, shipments, and efficiency increase: monitored through field records.

4.title: Valuation
4.text: Revenue, margins, and asset base strengthen; private-market value can be repriced.

5.title: Investor upside
5.text: Dividends, appreciation, or structured exit: campaign-specific; no guarantee.

valueTimeline.label: Institutional timeline
valueTimeline.title: From dossier review to liquidity: a disciplined journey
valueTimeline.lead: Each stage is documented. No time constraint; serious investment requires due diligence and operational follow-through.

1.title: Dossier review
1.text: Sector, capacity, export, and capital plan are documented in the dossier: a business model, not speculation.

2.title: Due diligence
2.text: Six-layer review: documents, field, finance, legal, founders, committee: rejected companies are not published.

3.title: Capital allocation
3.text: Participation via licensed platform; funds remain in escrow until the campaign closes.

4.title: Operational growth
4.text: Machine commissioning, capacity increase, new lines: measurable progress in the real economy.

5.title: Export scaling
5.text: New markets, shipment volume, customer diversification: the growth engine of a productive company.

6.title: Profitability
6.text: Margins and cash flow develop through operational discipline; investors follow via reports.

7.title: Value appreciation
7.text: Company value may be reassessed against production and profitability: no market guarantee.

8.title: Exit / liquidity
8.text: Dividends, strategic acquisition, secondary sale, or structured exit: stated in the information form; liquidity may be limited.

mechanics.label: Investment mechanics
mechanics.title: What you own: legally and operationally
mechanics.lead: Real financial infrastructure: regulated platform, custody, registry, and reporting. Not a crypto wallet , MKK-registered equity partnership.

1.title: Legal ownership
1.text: Upon campaign completion, shares registered in your name at MKK after capital increase , partnership in an industrial company not listed on the public exchange.

2.title: SPK framework
2.text: Equity-based partnership through an SPK-listed licensed crowdfunding platform: individual investor protection rules apply.

3.title: Escrow / custody
3.text: Funds remain in escrow until the campaign closes; refund process if target is not met. No direct transfer to the company.

4.title: After investment
4.text: MKK registration, partnership notification, information form commitments: investor transitions to shareholder status.

5.title: Reporting
5.text: Company updates, field records, operational traces: production reality shared, not speculative price.

6.title: Returns and exit
6.text: Dividends, appreciation, or structured exit vary by campaign. No return guarantee; you can lose money.

conviction.title: Why productive assets?

1.title: Understandable economics
1.text: Factory, machinery, export, and capacity: tracked with concrete metrics; not abstract token speculation.

2.title: Durable value layer
2.text: The foundation of production and employment; cyclical yet carrying real assets and operational cash flow.

3.title: Access discipline
3.text: A layer historically closed to funds and networks; ORTAQ offers access at institutional standard through dossiers, review, and verification.

conviction.disclaimer: Not investment advice. Past operational performance does not guarantee future returns.

ownership.label: Partnership model
ownership.title: In 60 seconds: what do you own?
ownership.lead: Not speculation: equity in a real company. Capital works in the production economy.

1.title: Share ownership
1.text: When the campaign completes, shares registered in your name at MKK: partnership in an industrial company not listed on the public exchange.

2.title: Role of capital
2.text: Your money funds capacity expansion, machine investment, export infrastructure, or operational bottlenecks , stated clearly in each dossier.

3.title: How value forms
3.text: Production volume, export share, margins, and company value can grow through operational improvement. The investor owns a share of that table.

4.title: Return expectations
4.text: Dividends, appreciation, or strategic exit: specified in the campaign information form. No return guarantee; you can lose money.

wealth.barrier: Due diligence cost, closed networks, and fragmented process kept this layer away from individual investors. ORTAQ makes access visible through disciplined dossiers and verification.

why.label: Why it matters
why.title: Most people cannot access the productive economy.
why.lead: In Turkey, individual capital mostly flows to financial assets. Yet employment, export, and capacity are built in factories.
why.fewLabel: What almost no one can access
why.barrier: This layer stayed opaque: due diligence is costly, access is closed, process is fragmented. Outside large funds and local networks, individual participation is nearly impossible.
why.ortaq: ORTAQ makes this layer visible through dossiers, field inspection, and verification discipline , without rushing, transparently.

masthead.subline: Not the stock market; factory reality. Field verification, capacity, export profile, and risk records in one evaluation layer.
masthead.disclaimer: ORTAQ is the evaluation infrastructure under SPK regulation. Transactions are not yet open · dossiers update live.

visual.evidence: Imagery is not campaign marketing; industrial atmosphere selected for sector and field context.

masthead.thesis: Passive assets wait for price. Production companies compound value through capacity, exports, and operational improvement , equity owners share in that growth.

marketLayer.label: Market layer
marketLayer.title: Private industrial capital was always there; access was not.
marketLayer.lead: Most production companies in Turkey are private. Ownership exists, but credible access historically required institutional networks and costly due diligence.

points.1: The constraint is not interest: it is verification cost, fragmented signals, and inconsistent disclosure.
points.2: Operational growth is not speculation: capacity, exports, margins, and bottlenecks are measurable on the factory floor.
points.3: A durable market layer needs one thing: comparable evidence and an auditable process across companies.

marketLayer.footnote: Infrastructure framing: not an investment offer.

mechanics.label: Mechanics
mechanics.title: Access mechanics in one page
mechanics.lead: Regulated participation, private-market ownership, and possible realization paths , stated as mechanisms, not promises.

1.title: Dossier discipline
1.body: Capacity, export profile, field notes, and risk layers: per company, in one evaluable file.

2.title: Evidence standard
2.body: Not paper-only. The physical trace of production: line, shifts, dispatch, bottlenecks, is verified on-site and recorded.

3.title: Regulated participation
3.body: SPK-licensed platform, escrow account, MKK share registry. Funds are not transferred to the company until the campaign closes.

4.title: Valuation arithmetic
4.body: Equity, valuation, and growth assumptions are explicit. Value can change with capacity, margin, and exports , never guaranteed.

mechanics.ownership: When participation completes, shares are registered in your name after capital increase. This is private-market equity, not exchange trading.
mechanics.growth: Operational improvement can affect company value through economics: throughput, quality stability, export mix, and margin resilience.

1.title: Secondary sale
1.body: Transfer of existing shares to another buyer; price and timing depend on market conditions.

2.title: Strategic investor
2.body: Entry of a corporate or sector buyer; usually linked to company maturity and scale.

3.title: Company buyback
3.body: The business repurchases specific shares; depends on cash position and governance decisions.

4.title: Future funding rounds
4.body: New capital entry or structured exit; valuation shaped by company performance and terms.

mechanics.liquidity: None of these pathways is guaranteed. Secondary liquidity may be limited; horizon and timing depend on company, market, and regulatory conditions.

marketWhy.label: Why this market exists
marketWhy.body: Private production assets dominate the real economy, but access stayed institution-only. A standardized evidence rail is the missing layer between capital and factories.

mechanics.footnote: Not investment advice · no return or liquidity guarantee

mechanism.label: Mechanism
mechanism.title: In 60 seconds: what you buy and how it works
mechanism.lead: Not a speculation layer: equity-partnership infrastructure in production companies.
mechanism.thesis: Passive assets wait for price. Production companies compound value through capacity, exports, and operational improvement , equity owners share in that growth.

1.title: Dossier review
1.body: Capacity, export profile, field notes, and risk layers: a per-company evaluation file.

2.title: Verification discipline
2.body: Not paper-only. The physical trace of production: line, shifts, dispatch, bottlenecks, is verified on-site and recorded.

3.title: Regulated participation
3.body: SPK-licensed platform, escrow account, MKK share registry. Funds are not transferred to the company until the campaign closes.

4.title: Valuation arithmetic
4.body: Equity, valuation, and growth assumptions are explicit. Value accrues through capacity, margin, and exports , never guaranteed.

mechanism.karatExample: Example: In Karat Parça (Konya), value is read through a chain: new CNC line → higher capacity → export dispatch → margin improvement. This is not a promise; it is operational accounting.
mechanism.footnote: Not investment advice · transactions are not yet open

realization.label: Capital structure
realization.title: Ownership, value, and realization
realization.lead: ORTAQ is not an exchange. The investor reads field-verified equity-partnership dossiers on production companies with MKK-registered shares; realization depends on the campaign and company structure.
realization.ownership: When participation completes, shares are registered in your name after capital increase. This is not a public-market purchase; it is private-market equity.
realization.growth: Value is tied to measurable operational improvement at the plant: capacity, exports, margins, machine efficiency. Company economics, not speculative price.

1.title: Secondary sale
1.body: Transfer of existing shares to another buyer; price and timing depend on market conditions.

2.title: Strategic investor
2.body: Entry of a corporate or sector buyer; usually linked to company maturity and scale.

3.title: Company buyback
3.body: The business repurchases specific shares; depends on cash and management decision.

4.title: Future funding rounds
4.body: New capital entry or structured exit; valuation round shaped by company performance.

realization.liquidity: None of these pathways is guaranteed. Secondary liquidity is limited; horizon and exit timing depend on company, market, and regulatory conditions.

marketWhy.label: Why this market exists
marketWhy.body: Most of Turkey's production economy still consists of private companies. Individual investors historically could not access this layer , due diligence cost, closed networks, fragmented process. ORTAQ aims to close that structural access gap through dossier discipline; it does not create a speculation layer.

realization.footnote: Not investment advice · no return or liquidity guarantee

process.label: How it works
process.title: From visibility to qualified introduction
process.intro: A company profile is built and verified; investment readiness is scored; direct, verified access is provided to the capital partner that fits the thesis. ORTAQ is the validation and introduction layer; it holds no money.
process.journeyNote: ORTAQ provides the validation and readiness layer; introductions happen on mutual consent; ORTAQ holds no money.

1.title: Visibility
1.text: The company becomes a standard, investor-friendly, comparable profile.

2.title: Verification
2.text: Export, capacity and documents are verified on the ground and with data.

3.title: Readiness
3.text: Investment readiness is measured and scored; gaps are identified.

4.title: Match
4.text: Suitable capital partners are identified by sector, geography, ticket size and thesis.

5.title: Introduction
5.text: On mutual interest, a qualified introduction is made; the process continues between the parties.

journey.label: Process
journey.note: Visibility → verification → readiness → introduction. ORTAQ is a step-by-step documented validation and access layer.

evaluationPage.label: How we select
evaluationPage.title: How do we select companies?
evaluationPage.lead: Not every application is accepted. ORTAQ makes only producing, verifiable companies with clear growth logic investment-ready, through multi-stage verification before they reach a capital partner.
evaluationPage.leadSecondary: This is the readiness leg of access: after visibility, before introduction. The trust framework and introduction are built on it.
evaluationPage.journeyContext: After verification, read the trust framework, then move to the access process.

1.title: Initial screening
1.text: Sector fit, operating history, production or export proof, basic financials.
1.reject: One-person consultancies, speculative projects, undocumented claims.

2.title: Operational review
2.text: Factory or production site visit, machine park, order book, supply chain.

3.title: Legal and registry
3.text: Trade registry, MERSİS, tax compliance, ownership structure, litigation search.

4.title: Financial review
4.text: Revenue trend, debt structure, cash flow, capital use plan consistency.

5.title: Founder interview
5.text: In-person or video interview. Operational knowledge, goals, risk awareness.
5.reject: Misleading statements, press pressure, unrealistic growth claims.

6.title: Readiness approval
6.text: Validation and readiness review is completed; the company is deemed fit to present to a capital partner.

trustPage.title: Trust and compliance
trustPage.subtitle: ORTAQ holds no money, sells no investments and gives no advice. It is a validation, readiness and qualified introduction layer; evidence and transparency at every step.
trustPage.journeyNote: After reading the verification process, see the trust layers here, then move to the access process.

regulation.label: Regulation
regulation.body: Investment under SPK framework via licensed crowdfunding platform; ORTAQ is the preparation layer.

1.a: No. ORTAQ is growth and matching infrastructure. Investment is opened at the licensed partner.

2.a: The investor side is aimed at persons and institutions that meet qualified investor and campaign rules.

3.a: Two structures: contractual revenue share or equity. Rate and timing are in the deal contract; no return guarantee.

4.a: No. Outcomes depend on the company's commercial performance and contract terms.

5.a: Regulated steps run at the licensed partner. ORTAQ handles file preparation and commercial growth.

6.a: Export and operations evidence are collected first to reduce investor risk.

7.a: Partner suggestions, reliability indicator, export steps, and investor file.

8.a: Use the application form as a producer, investor, or partner.

regulatory.label: Compliance
regulatory.title: Money does not sit at ORTAQ; regulated institutions protect you.
regulatory.lead: Common question: is money collected here? No. Roles are split clearly.

ortaq.duty: Matching and dossiers. No advice or execution.

partner.duty: Campaign, escrow, and execution on the licensed platform.

points.1: How ORTAQ earns: it takes no commission from investors and collects no funds. It earns a service fee when a company it prepares advances through the process.
points.2: Money flow: the investor's money goes to the escrow account at the licensed institution, then to the company. ORTAQ never touches it.
points.3: Licensed partner structure: the campaign, escrow account and execution open on the SPK licensed platform; share registration follows MKK discipline.
points.4: Funds are processed at the licensed partner per campaign terms.
points.5: For equity structures, MKK registration follows the licensed process.

regulatory.disclaimer: For information only. Current contract and regulation apply.

why.title: Why ORTAQ?
why.subtitle: Selling abroad is slow, costly, and uncertain for most producers.

items.1: A fair gives visibility once, the rest is on you
items.2: A consultant charges whether you win or not
items.3: On your own, which buyer is real stays unclear for months
items.4: On Alibaba and B2B marketplaces you are on your own, buyers are not pre-screened

items.1: The right buyer is found beforehand
items.2: You are matched with a trusted partner

1.title: A real industrial company
1.body: A Turkish manufacturer, not listed. Equity or revenue share.

2.title: Proven growth
2.body: Export and capacity verified on site. One dossier, clear picture.

3.title: Deal at licensed partner
3.body: Funds do not sit at ORTAQ. The partnership opens on a licensed platform.

lines.1: There are good manufacturers. There are buyers in the world. The problem is they cannot find each other.
lines.2: The product is ready, the factory is ready, the capacity is ready.
lines.3: A manufacturer that reaches the right buyer grows. ORTAQ speeds up this process.
lines.4: The right match starts not a single sale but sustainable growth.

whyNow.label: Why now
whyNow.text: Many buyers worldwide are trying to move production outside China. Türkiye is more visible than it has been in years. Manufacturers that open to the right markets in this period can gain a meaningful advantage.

moneyFlow.label: Money flow
moneyFlow.title: How the money moves, and how it returns

out.2: Escrow in the an authorized, regulated institution

moneyFlow.note: ORTAQ is the matching, preparation and dossier layer. It never touches the money.

founder.label: Founder
founder.title: Who runs this?
founder.name: Harun Kaya
founder.bio: 15+ years of experience in exports, international sales and distributor development. Led field sales at LEGO Group across Türkiye, Israel, Greece and the CIS region. Spent years seeing on the ground that manufacturers could not grow because they could not reach the right buyers. ORTAQ was founded to solve this problem.
founder.status: We are currently running pilot conversations with a first group of manufacturers.

dossier.label: Sample dossier
dossier.title: What does an investment dossier look like?
dossier.note: This is a sample, not real company data.

investorValue.label: For capital partners
investorValue.title: How does a capital partner earn?

1.v: You receive a share of the financed manufacturer's sales at the contracted rate.

2.v: Total repayment is limited by a cap set in the contract in advance.

3.v: Money returns automatically from sales; no IPO or exit event is required.

4.v: ORTAQ verifies and prepares dossiers; execution runs through a an authorized, regulated institution and collects no funds.

investorValue.disclaimer: Not investment advice, no return guarantee.

inThirty.label: ORTAQ in 30 seconds
inThirty.title: The system at a glance

1.v: A manufacturer with an export order or clear growth potential.

2.v: We verify the order and commercial progress on the ground and turn it into a financing-ready dossier.

3.v: Collateral-free, revenue-share based growth capital is provided.

4.v: Repayment is made from the company's sales, up to the cap in the contract.

5.v: The manufacturer grows; the capital partner shares in that growth.

inThirty.earn: ORTAQ earns a service fee in this process. It takes no collateral from manufacturers, no commission from partners, and collects no funds.

hero.headline: We make strong companies the right capital cannot see visible.
hero.sub: We make exporting and growing manufacturers investment-ready and visible, so the right capital partners find you. We do not sell investments, we are not a fund.

problem.label: The access gap
problem.title: The problem is not capital, it is access.
problem.body: Investors see thousands of companies, companies see thousands of investors. The problem is not seeing; it is that the right company and the right capital never reach each other. The best growth manufacturers stay invisible to the right capital, outside relationship and region-bound traditional networks.

broken.label: Why it fails
broken.title: Why does traditional capital access fail?

1.v: Good companies do not enter the right capital's radar in a standard way.

2.v: Traditional networks are limited by region and relationships; they do not cover this segment.

3.v: Capital examines each opportunity from scratch, so most never get evaluated.

engine.label: Capital Access Engine
engine.title: ORTAQ is one access system, five layers.
engine.intro: Not marketing sections; real product layers that make a company accessible to the right capital.

1.v: Turns a company into a standard, investor-friendly, comparable opportunity.

2.v: Measures and scores investment readiness. Not consulting, a scalable system.

3.v: Structured access to the right capital by sector, geography, ticket size and thesis.

4.v: Qualified, double opt-in introductions. ORTAQ holds no money.

5.v: Every interaction makes the system smarter. This is the moat.

readiness.label: Readiness Engine
readiness.title: A score that measures financeability.
readiness.body: The most important differentiator. Not a hand-written report, a scalable scoring system. Capital understands opportunity quality at a glance; the company sees what to improve.
readiness.scoreNote: Sample score. Not real company data.

matching.label: Matching Engine
matching.title: Not more matches, the right access.
matching.body: The goal is not the number of introductions but qualified, low-cost access to verified companies that fit your thesis.

forCompanies.label: For companies
forCompanies.title: Reach the right capital.

points.1: We turn your company into a single profile investors can easily understand.
points.2: With a readiness score you see where you stand and what to improve.
points.3: We make qualified introductions to the right capital partners.

forCompanies.problem: You have orders and growth, but the right capital does not see you.

forCapital.label: For capital partners
forCapital.title: See verified growth opportunities that normally only large funds can access.

points.1: Two capital profiles: institutional capital (private equity, growth funds, family offices, strategic investors) and qualified private capital (successful entrepreneurs, business owners, experienced investors).
points.2: You reach Türkiye's growing, exporting manufacturers, pre-checked and with a readiness score. You skip starting due diligence from scratch and save time.
points.3: How you earn: you take equity or provide financing; your return comes from the company's growth (dividends, value appreciation or revenue share). Terms are set between you and the company; ORTAQ is not a party, it makes the qualified introduction.

forCapital.problem: Finding and vetting strong but unknown manufacturers takes months; most never reach you. ORTAQ is building a capital network, not a fund directory.

intelligence.label: Intelligence Layer
intelligence.title: The network gets smarter over time.
intelligence.body: ORTAQ is not an AI company; it is a capital access company that gets smarter with proprietary data accumulating in this segment. That data is an advantage no generic network has.

points.1: Which capital is interested in which company
points.3: Which sector matches which capital type

points.1: Holds no money, transfers none, conducts no securities activity.
points.2: Gives no investment advice and promises no returns.
points.3: It is only an access, readiness and qualified introduction layer.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Clear answers

1.a: No. It holds no money and sells no shares. It is only an access and qualified introduction layer.

2.a: Through access and intelligence subscriptions: companies pay for readiness and visibility, capital partners for qualified opportunity access.

3.a: No. No funds are collected from retail investors; qualified introductions are made with institutional capital partners.

4.a: A scoring system that measures a company's investment readiness, not a hand-written consulting report.

faq.lead: Clear answers on access, readiness and process.

cta.label: Get started
cta.title: Start from the right side.
cta.body: If you are a strong company, become visible and investment-ready; if you are a capital partner, see opportunities your network cannot reach.

invisible.label: Why they are invisible
invisible.title: Why are these companies not already visible?

1.v: Most strong exporters focus on production and operations, not fundraising.

2.v: Traditional investment networks never see a large part of the market.

3.v: Being visible and being capital-ready are not the same thing.

4.v: ORTAQ closes this gap: it makes them visible, ready, and accessible to the right capital.

1.v: For exporting and growing manufacturers, and the capital partners who want to reach them.

2.v: Makes a company investment-ready and visible, and provides qualified introductions to the right capital.

3.v: Strong companies and the right capital often never reach each other through traditional channels. ORTAQ builds that access.

how.label: How it works
how.title: How it works

1.v: The company becomes a standard, comparable profile.

2.v: Investment readiness is measured and scored. The common language capital trusts is built here.

3.v: Structured access to verified capital partners that fit the thesis.

4.v: Qualified introductions on mutual interest. ORTAQ holds no money.

how.scoreNote: Sample score. Not real company data.
how.note: The system gets smarter with every match.
how.engineNote: The score is produced automatically from this data: a repeatable, scalable system, not a hand-written report. Humans are involved only in verification.

model.label: Business model
model.title: How does ORTAQ earn?

ortaq.v: Both sides pay a subscription: companies for readiness and visibility, capital partners for access to verified deal flow. No commission on investments, no money held.

company.v: It becomes visible and investment-ready, and reaches the right capital faster, with fewer barriers.

investor.v: Access to verified, ready opportunities; lower discovery and due-diligence time and risk.

model.align: ORTAQ only earns when it produces genuinely investment-ready companies and genuine qualified access. So its incentive is aligned with both sides.
model.note: A subscription, not a commission. Revenue comes from access and data, so ORTAQ only earns when it produces real value.
